Zoom in / out toward the cursor (Shift = larger steps).
Change thickness of the line under the cursor (or hovered point’s line).
Rotate the selected line around its center — or the focused point if one is selected.
Pan the canvas; over a point/segment it drags that instead.
Reset zoom to fit the window.
Begin or finish drawing the current line.
Switch between polyline drawing and rectangle drawing.
Add a point to the line being drawn.
Close the shape into a locked, fillable area.
Sweep out a rectangle: 4 corner points, a locked area.
Add a new point; connects to the selected line/point if one is selected.
Insert a new point between the two connecting points.
Select the line and focus the clicked point.
Edit color, thickness, marker size and style; locked areas also get a fill option.
Pick a fill color for a selected closed area; the ✕ button clears it back to transparent.
Edit point coordinates (double-click) or remove points.
Show coordinates of the nearest point or line endpoints.
Show every point of the hovered line (updates instantly).
Set default line styles, area fill and highlight colors.
